## Summary

**At the Time of Purchase (October 13, 2020):**

Groshans had not played in a game in 2020 due to the cancellation of the minor league season, but card investors would have seen a highly ranked prospect recovering from a foot injury. At Toronto's alternate site in 2020, he led all Jays hitters in home runs, and as a 2018 first-round (12th overall) pick from the Toronto Blue Jays, he represented a solid prospect with a long frame with an athletic, well-sequenced swing and an advanced offensive approach, with good bat speed and above-average raw power. The 2020 Bowman cards for Groshans were new product hitting the market that fall.

**What Happened After:**

The investment failed despite initial promise. While Groshans batted .291/.367/.450 with seven homers at Double-A New Hampshire in 2021, ranked as the 55th overall prospect, the 21-year-old missed time with back soreness in May and was restricted to the IL for all of September for undisclosed reasons. Most critically, when he made his MLB debut in 2022, he spent the full 2023 season at Triple-A Jacksonville posting
